description: This set of phase diagrams illustrates the effects of Al and Mn contents on κ-carbide solvus temperatures in Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels. The first subplot targets Fe-30Mn-xAl-1C alloys, with x-axis representing Al content (5-10 wt%) and y-axis representing temperature (500-900 °C), marking three phase regions: single γ (austenite), γ+κ* (γ + intergranular κ-carbide), and γ+κ*+κ' (γ + intergranular + intragranular κ-carbide). The solid and dashed lines are solvus boundaries, which rise significantly as Al content increases. The second subplot targets Fe-xMn-7Al-1C alloys, with x-axis representing Mn content (23-29 wt%), showing the same three phase regions, while solvus boundaries decrease slightly as Mn content increases. Experimental data points (cross, open circle, filled circle) validate the plotted boundaries. This figure confirms that Al addition substantially elevates both intergranular and intragranular κ-carbide solvus temperatures, while Mn addition causes mild reduction, providing guidance for composition and heat treatment design of Fe-Mn-Al-C steels.
